import io
import string
import list

fn parse_list(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result = []
  mut current = ""
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    let b = match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(v)
      v
    when None
      0
    end
    if b == 32 or b == 9
      if not string.is_empty(current)
        match string.parse_int(current)
        when Some(v)
          result = list.push(result, v)
        when None
          ()
        end
        current = ""
      end
    else
      current = "#{current}#{string.from_byte(b)}"
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if not string.is_empty(current)
    match string.parse_int(current)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn get_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ i: Int) -> Int
  match list.get(xs, i)
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end
end

fn bsearch(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = list.len(xs) - 1
  mut found: Option<Int> = None
  mut done = false
  while not done and lo <= hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    let v = get_at(xs, mid)
    if v == t
      found = Some(mid)
      done = true
    else if v < t
      lo = mid + 1
    else
      hi = mid - 1
    end
  end
  found
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let xs = parse_list(line1)
let t = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
when Some(v)
  v
when None
  0
end

match bsearch(xs, t)
when Some(i)
  print("index=#{i}")
when None
  print("not_found")
end
